steps.title: Identify the opening question
steps.content: The passage opens with a direct question: “Why do rusty-spotted cats purr but jaguars roar?” This question about the difference between two species defines what the text will address.

steps.title: Match that structure to the choices
steps.content: The passage then develops an explanation answering that question—a hypothesis about the hyoid bone that accounts for the behavioral difference. Choice C describes this structure exactly: posing a question about two species, then presenting a possible answer. A, B, and D misidentify the text's content or structure—A discusses habitat changes never mentioned, B reverses the relationship between theory and supporting facts, and D treats the contrasted behaviors as shared. That matches choice C — C is correct.
